Bioregional Weaving Alliance Ireland

network_affiliation
[[Bioregional Weaving Labs]]
type
organization
location_lat
52.3
background_section
## Background The Bride Valley, which is part of the Copper Coast UNESCO Geopark, is characterized by a primarily agricultural landscape—a mix of dairy farms, grazing lands, and areas being transformed for nature conservation. Multiple generations of family farms form the backbone of the local economy, creating a context where sustainable transitions must work with existing farming communities rather than replacing them. ### Origins and Network The Bioregional Weaving Alliance Ireland (BWA Ireland) is a bioregional initiative functioning as a transition hub, learning centre, and living lab. Established as a [non-profit in 2022](https://youtu.be/i5GEa804rO0), the organization brings together a network of Irish stakeholders, partners, and the living systems of the region itself. The organization is involved in a 'deep demonstration' partnership with the Irish government to test landscape approaches to the future of sustainable dairy, positioning them to establish the Bioregional Weaving Alliance as an entity designed to support learning and initiatives leading to bioregional regeneration, restoration, and protection, especially in the South East.
Sources
33314415-10e1-8146-b3f7-cb934434b784, 33314415-10e1-818c-b665-f1772721b5c4
patterns

place

activities

description
BWL South East Ireland is a bioregional initiative functioning as a transition hub, learning centre, and living lab. It brings together a network of Irish stakeholders, partners, and the living systems of the region itself. Established as a non-profit in 2022 and formally constituted in 2024.
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy Through their deep demonstration partnership with the Irish government and their portfolio of initiatives, BWA Ireland is positioned to serve as a vessel for change—inspiring farmers, communities, and visitors to participate in regenerative practices that benefit nature, farmers, economy, and inspiration. The work demonstrates how locally-driven initiatives can scale up through policy influence (as with the FBI model), while maintaining strong community roots and collaboration at the bioregional level.
website
https://bioregion.ie/
bioregional_context
The bioregion is centered around the Bride Valley in Ireland and includes the village of Dunhill, which is part of the Copper Coast UNESCO Geopark. The landscape is primarily agricultural, with a mix of dairy farms, grazing lands, and areas being transformed for nature conservation.
category
Bioregional Organizer
